briefDescription: We exploit CyTOF and identify specific immune signatures in (PBMCs from the early-stage HCC patients and dynamic immune signatures to assess the responsiveness of anti-PD-1 advanced HCC patients .
briefTitle: Single-Cell immune signature for detecting early-stage HCC and early assessing PD-1 immunotherapy efficacy
description: We exploit single-cell mass cytometry and identify stage-specific immune signatures in peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) from the early-stage HCC patients. We also reveal dynamic single-cell immune signatures of PBMCs to directly assess the final responsiveness of anti-PD-1 monotherapy treated advanced HCC patients one year later.
hypothesis: Single-cell immune signatures could provide promising non-invasive approaches for clinical early detection of HCC and early assessment and prediction of anti-PD-1 immunotherapy efficacy in advanced HCC patients.
objectives: to find single-cell immune signatures that provide for early detection of HCC and early prediction of anti-PD-1 immunotherapy efficacy in advanced HCC patients.
officialTitle: Single-Cell immune signature for detecting early-stage HCC and early assessing PD-1 immunotherapy efficacy
sponsoringOrganization: Natural Science Foundation of Zhejiang Province
